items.correct: It failed to overthrow the Tsar but forced the October Manifesto creating the Duma, exposing autocracy's weaknesses and rehearsing the structures of 1917.
items.misconception: The 1905 Revolution achieved nothing because the Tsar stayed in power.
items.random_wrong: The 1905 Revolution was led by Lenin from the Winter Palace.
items.why: Lenin was in exile during 1905; the leadership was diverse. Trotsky called it 'the dress rehearsal' — failure in 1905 created conditions for 1917.

items.q: Why did the Provisional Government fail in 1917?
items.correct: It continued the unpopular war, did not redistribute land, shared 'dual power' with the Petrograd Soviet, and was discredited by the Kornilov affair before the Bolshevik takeover.
items.misconception: The Provisional Government fell because Lenin was personally more charismatic than Kerensky.
items.random_wrong: The Provisional Government fell because the Tsar returned and reclaimed his throne.
items.why: Its failure was structural, not personal: war, land and dual power. Students reduce political collapse to charismatic leadership when material grievances drove events.

items.q: How did the Bolsheviks seize power in October 1917?
items.correct: Trotsky's Military Revolutionary Committee coordinated a near-bloodless takeover of key buildings in Petrograd from a Provisional Government that had lost army and worker support.
items.misconception: The October Revolution was a mass popular uprising that stormed the Winter Palace.
items.random_wrong: The October Revolution overthrew the Tsar.
items.why: It was a coordinated coup with limited fighting; the Tsar had been gone since February. Soviet propaganda mythologised it as a mass storming, which film cemented.

items.q: In what month and year were the serfs emancipated?
items.why: It's February 1861. “March 1881” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: What name was given to the local councils created in 1864?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Zemstva. “Soviets” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In what year did Bloody Sunday occur?
items.why: The real figure is 1905. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
